An adversarial pass found a privilege escalation in my own merged work

Five PRs merged to main with zero code review, because Claude PR Review is gated on a needs-claude-review label none of them carried. Running the assessment afterwards surfaced 29 findings. This is what they were, and what is fixed.

5PRs merged unreviewed
11high-severity findings
8confirmed after refutation
1refuted, not acted on

Assessment scores

correctness6.0
security6.0
maintainability6.0
simplicity5.5
testability4.0

The attack chain I shipped

Code execution under the Release Bot token

A branch name was treated as a trust signal. Only main is protected, so release-please--* is pushable by any write-access actor.

gh pr list --jq 'select(.headRefName | startswith("release-please--"))'
git checkout "$BRANCH"           # untrusted tree
node scripts/stamp-whats-new.mjs # ...executed FROM it, App token in scope

The checkout also omitted persist-credentials: false — alone among 38 checkouts in this repo that set it — writing the token into .git/config where that same branch's code could read it.

Fix

Copy the stamper out of main before any checkout and run it via a new WHATS_NEW_ROOT override, so executed code is always trusted; require the release PR's author to be a bot; stop persisting credentials; authenticate the single push explicitly; and emit ::warning:: on every failure path instead of falling through to a success-looking no-op.

A wildcard that was a write primitive

RuleAlso matches
Bash(gh api repos/:owner/:repo/milestones:*) gh api … milestones -X POST | PATCH | DELETE

A trailing :* is a plain string-prefix match with no knowledge of gh's verb flags. The job holds issues: write and reads an attacker-influenceable issue body — exactly the threat the allowlist was added to contain. My comment claimed it held "nothing that can mutate the repo." That was false. Both rules are now exact, with no wildcard.

Claims I had overstated

ClaimedActually
demos-validate "validates pre-merge" Not in main's 17 required contexts — surfaces breakage, does not gate a merge
"audits all four trees" npm resolves advisories only at registry.npmjs.org, so @yonatan-hq/* on GitHub Packages has no coverage
What's New parser is correct An unparseable ## header (e.g. 8.85.0-rc.1) silently merged its bullets into the previous release

Verified, not asserted

CheckResult
actionlint (3 workflows)clean
stamp-whats-new --checkstill passes
WHATS_NEW_ROOT external roottrusted-copy path works
synthetic prerelease changelogrc bullet no longer leaks into v8.84.7

The lesson is not "an agent found bugs". It is that all five PRs were green — CI Assessment scored them 91/100 Grade A — while the coverage number behind that grade (83.3%, hooks only) measured none of the shell, mjs or YAML actually shipped. Green CI and a review are different things.
